title: Configure Google Sheet Delivery
description: Export squid run results directly to a shared Google Sheet

content.introduction:
Configure Google Sheet delivery to export run results directly to a shared Google Sheet. Results can be overwritten with each run or appended with deduplication.

## Behavior

- **append = false**: Only the latest run's data is exported. Any previously exported data is overwritten.
- **append = true**: Data from all runs is appended and deduplicated before export.

## Requirements

- The Google Sheet must be shared with "Anyone with the link"
- The sheet must have **Editor** access permission
- Export is limited to 100,000 rows

parameters.name: google_sheet_fields.url
parameters.description: Public Google Sheet URL (must be accessible with Editor permission)

parameters.name: google_sheet_fields.append
parameters.description: false: Overwrites previous data with the last run's result. true: Appends new results, deduplicated

parameters.name: google_sheet_fields.is_active
parameters.description: Set to true to enable Google Sheet export, false to disable

fields.description: Whether Google Sheet export is active

parameters.name: squid
parameters.description: The unique identifier (hash) of the squid for which to configure Google Sheet delivery

proTips.content: The Google Sheet MUST be shared with 'Anyone with the link' and have Editor permissions. Without these settings, export will fail.
Use append: false for dashboards where you only want the latest data. Use append: true for historical tracking.
When append is true, Lobstr automatically deduplicates rows before exporting, so you won't get duplicate entries.
Export is limited to 100,000 rows. If your squid generates more results, consider using S3 or SFTP delivery instead.
Use the Test Google Sheet endpoint to verify your sheet permissions before activating automatic delivery.

seo.title: Configure Google Sheet Delivery - Lobstr.io API Documentation
seo.description: Learn how to export squid results directly to Google Sheets using the Lobstr.io API. Configure sheet URLs and append modes.
